narrative: OMX30 opens on strong European tailwinds with DAX and CAC leading at +2.5–3.2%, supported by a +1.813% weighted correlation signal and risk-on sentiment from Iran peace developments. Oil's -7.85% collapse is a net positive for industrials and consumer sectors, offsetting input cost inflation. However, technicals warn caution: the index sits +10.6% above its 200-MA, Stochastic K is overbought at 81.9, and MACD has turned negative (hist=-2.915). Weak ADX of 14.8 means trending conviction is low, requiring 30% confidence haircut. Yields falling sharply (-0.68% US 10Y) create a mixed environment—positive for equities but negative for bank NIM expansion. Expect a modest opening rally (+1.0–1.5%) but watch for profit-taking into overbought conditions.
morningBrief: OMX30 should gap up +1.0–1.5% at open, riding European strength and oil's geopolitical relief rally. Key watch: hold above 3123 (yesterday close); 3150–3180 is tough resistance tied to the 50-MA. Industrials (Atlas Copco, Volvo, Sandvik) lead on oil weakness and modest USD/SEK bid; mining (Boliden) is your best performer given gold +3.06% and copper +4.00%. Banks (SEB, Handelsbanken) are mixed—yields down -0.68% compresses NIM outlook despite equity strength. Tech (Ericsson, Hexagon) neutral to slightly weak on NASDAQ +1.03% and falling rate environment. Watch for Stochastic mean reversion and MACD signal cross; if RSI breaks above 70 (currently 54.45), flag exhaustion. US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) is your key afternoon event—risk asset sensitivity remains high. Trade the setup as a fade into resistance; book profits at 3170–3180.
drivers: Strong European equity momentum (DAX +2.49%, CAC +3.21%, FTSE +2.50%)
Oil selloff (-7.85%) on Iran peace deal hopes reduces input cost headwinds
Gold +3.06% and copper +4.00% boost mining/materials; weak ADX (14.8) requires caution
USD/SEK down -0.75%, EUR/SEK down -0.21% favors Swedish exporters moderately
Stochastic overbought (K=81.9) signals mean reversion risk; RSI 54.45 neutral but elevated

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil -7.85% reduces input cost headwinds significantly; modest USD strength aids export competitiveness.
Gold +3.06% and copper +4.00% drive direct revenue uplift; Boliden is top performer.
US 10Y yield down -0.68% to 4.416% narrows NIM expansion, offsetting risk-on equity gains.
NASDAQ +1.03% is modest; falling rates pressure DCF valuations; sentiment remains fragile.
Oil collapse improves consumer purchasing power; risk-on sentiment supports discretionary.
Falling yields reduce refinancing pressure but lower discount rates compress valuation multiples.
Defensive sectors neutral in risk-on environment; AstraZeneca exposed to healthcare policy risk.

watchList.reason: Gold +3.06% and copper +4.00% drive earnings uplift; strongest fundamental setup in OMX30 this morning.
Oil -7.85% crushes fuel costs; modestly bid USD/SEK supports export pricing; strong industrial demand backdrop.
Input cost relief from oil collapse; industrials leading; watch for Q2 forward guidance.
Risk-on sentiment and oil-driven consumer spending tailwind; sourcing cost pressure from EUR/SEK weak offset by demand lift.
Yields down -0.68% compress NIM; equity strength supports loan growth. Net effect muted; await earnings.
NASDAQ +1.03% is weak; falling rates compress growth multiples; geopolitical uncertainty pressures telecom M&A cycle.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims (14:30 CET) — critical for risk sentiment; expected 215K, any miss will boost equities further. No major Swedish data releases. ECB speakers this afternoon may reference rate path.
topRisk: Stochastic overbought (K=81.9) + weak ADX (14.8) + MACD negative cross = exhaustion pattern. Profit-taking into 3170–3180 is high probability; gap-and-fade scenarios should not be ruled out. Geopolitical escalation (Iran, Trump/Strait of Hormuz) could reverse oil and risk assets rapidly.
technicalSummary: Overbought: Stochastic K=81.9, Bollinger %B=0.80 in upper half
Momentum intact but deteriorating: ROC-90 +11.4%, but MACD below signal (hist=-2.915)
Trend strength weak: ADX 14.8 (below 18 threshold) — apply -30% confidence haircut
RSI 54.45 neutral but elevated; watch 60–70 zone for mean reversion signal
52-week position: 91% of range, -2.2% from high — limited upside cushion
